sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body:
To complete this tutorial, you must have already linked an IdP (Identity Provider) to
Atlas and mapped one or more domains to that IdP (Identity Provider).
For instructions on these procedures, see:

- [IdP to Atlas](atlas-manage-fed-auth)

- [Manage Domain Mapping for Federated Authentication](atlas-manage-fed-domain-map)

sections.heading: Map an Organization to your Identity Provider
sections.body:
> **Note:** Atlas creates an
> [Organization's IdP certificate is about to expire](<Organization's IdP certificate is about to expire>) alert
> automatically when you map an organization to an IdP (Identity Provider) provider. If
> you remove the mapping, Atlas deletes all instances of this
> alert.

- Click Link Organizations.

  Atlas displays all organizations where you are an
  [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>).

  Organizations which are not already connected to the Federation
  Application have Configure Access button in the
  Actions column.

- Click the desired organization's Configure Access button.

* On the Identity Provider screen, click
  Connect Identity Provider.

  Atlas directs you to the Identity Providers screen
  which shows all IdPs (Identity Providers) you have linked to Atlas.

* For the IdP (Identity Provider) you want to apply to the organization, click
  Connect.

* In the Apply Identity Provider to Organizations modal,
  select the organizations to which this IdP (Identity Provider) applies.

* Click Confirm.

- Click Linked Organizations in the left navigation.

- In the list of Organizations, ensure that your desired
  organizations now have the expected
  Identity Provider.

sections.heading: Change an Organization's Mapped Identity Provider
sections.body:
Reconfigure your IdP (Identity Provider) to change the organizations to which it's mapped.

- In Atlas, go to the Federation Management console for your organization.

  - If it's not already displayed, select your desired organization
    from the Organizations menu in the navigation bar.

  - In the sidebar, click Federation under
    the Identity & Access heading.

  - Click Open Federation Management App.

    The [Federation](https://cloud.mongodb.com/go?l=https%3A%2F%2Fcloud.mongodb.com%2Fv2%23%2Forg%2F%3Corganization%3E%2Ffederation) page
    displays.

- In the sidebar, click Organizations.

- Click the Identity Provider of the organization whose
  IdP (Identity Provider) you wish to change.

- Click Modify for the IdP (Identity Provider) which is currently mapped to
  the organization.

- At the bottom of the Edit Identity Provider form,
  deselect the organization.

- Click Next.

- Click Finish.

* Click Modify for the IdP (Identity Provider) you want to map to the
  organization.

* At the bottom of the Edit Identity Provider form,
  select the organization.

* Click Next.

* Click Finish.

sections.heading: (Optional) Configure Advanced Options for Your Organization
sections.body:
The following optional settings provide even greater control over user
management and authentication in your organization.

sections.heading: Assign a Default User Role for Your Organization
sections.body:
You can assign users who authenticate through the IdP (Identity Provider) a default
[organization role](organization-roles) in a mapped organization. Configuring
this option ensures that users who authenticate through your IdP (Identity Provider) have
the same set of permissions. This setting is not required for
organization mapping.

For instructions on assigning a default role, see
[Assign a Default User Role for an Organization](advanced-option-default-role).

> **Note:** The selected default role only applies to users who authenticate
> through the IdP (Identity Provider) if they do not have an Atlas role mapped to
> the IdP (Identity Provider) group already.

sections.heading: Restrict Access to an Organization by Domain
sections.body:
You can restrict access to your organization to an approved list of
domains. This allows you to set the domains from which organization
users can login without needing to directly map those domains to your
IdP (Identity Provider).

For instructions on restricting access by domain, see
[Restrict Access to an Organization by Domain](restrict-org-access-by-domain).

sections.heading: Disconnect an Organization from the Federation Application
sections.body:
When you disconnect an organization from the Federation Application,
Atlas no longer grants membership or a default
[organization role](organization-roles) to users who authenticate
through the IdP (Identity Provider).

From the **Federation Management Console**:

- In Atlas, go to the Federation Management console for your organization.

  - If it's not already displayed, select your desired organization
    from the Organizations menu in the navigation bar.

  - In the sidebar, click Federation under
    the Identity & Access heading.

  - Click Open Federation Management App.

    The [Federation](https://cloud.mongodb.com/go?l=https%3A%2F%2Fcloud.mongodb.com%2Fv2%23%2Forg%2F%3Corganization%3E%2Ffederation) page
    displays.

- Click Organizations.
